observations.summary: Phantom halts recur for a 5th consecutive story (4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.6, 4.8): update-project-context fired a halt with outcomeHistory ["COMPLETE"] and no question in the responseExcerpt or trace; the halt is correlated with an opencode timeout (rc:124) on the same step.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for update-project-context (halts: 1, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt is a normal pattern audit summary starting 'Here is my review summary for Story 4.8.' with no question). traces/4.8-a1-20260713T131507/update-project-context.json — 118 parts; --grep for 'HALT|halt|question|timeout|escalat|decision' returns 0 matches across all text parts; --narrative (14 of 118 shown) shows a coherent flow with no question or decision escalation. runner-errors.jsonl line 9: {"ts":"2026-07-13T16:11:47Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"Update project context (4.8) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes"}. Ledger entries 70 (4.1, hypothesized), 75 (4.2, confirmed), 82 (4.3, confirmed), 4.6 (2026-07-13T11:15:48.881Z, confirmed with traces) — 5th consecutive story with phantom halts.

observations.summary: The update-project-context step's opencode runner timed out (rc:124) with salvaged output — a new step joining the timeout list (previously prepare-tests, review-code, unit-tests, test-review, implement-story, NFR review); the 4.5h duration is 16x the step average and is the maxMs in trends.
observations.evidence: runner-errors.jsonl line 9: {"ts":"2026-07-13T16:11:47Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"Update project context (4.8) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes","stderrTail":""}. Journal step_end for update-project-context (status: success, durationMs: 16385095 — the maxMs in trends, 16x the 988592ms average; halts: 1; outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"]; responseExcerpt is a complete coherent summary). The timeout fired at 16:11:47Z (1.5h after step_start at 14:41:46Z) but step_end was at 19:14:51Z (3h after the timeout), suggesting the step continued or was retried after the salvaged output. Ledger entries record prior timeouts on prepare-tests (4.2, 4.3), review-code (3.9), unit-tests (3.11), test-review (4.4), implement-story (4.5), NFR review (4.4).

observations.summary: The 4.3 update_step to implement-story is working — Story 4.8's own test file was clean with no stale markers; the 3 markers found by review-tests are from earlier stories (2.5, SPIKE, sse-back-pressure), not from implement-story.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: 'Story 4.8 Scope: deploy-failure-recovery.spec.ts — 31 tests, all active, all passing. No skips, no stale markers, no empty stubs.' Journal step_end for implement-story: 'removed TDD phase markers' from its own test file and ATDD checklist. _bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-reviews/test-review-validation-4-8.md:104-108 confirms Story 4.8's test file had 0 skipped tests and 0 stale markers. The 3 markers fixed by review-tests (test-review-validation-4-8.md:118-122) are from earlier stories: artifacts/page.test.tsx:9 ('GREEN PHASE:' from Story 2.5), repo-size.spec.ts:13 ('Red-phase status:' from a SPIKE), sse-back-pressure.spec.ts:37 ('red-phase coordination gap'). Playbook implement-story prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-12T15:50:56.335Z, updatedByRun: 4.3-a1-20260712T082910) includes 'remove all transitional phase markers from test-file headers and test planning artifacts (such as ATDD checklists) — any comment, header, or status line that describes a TDD phase transition (whether claiming tests are skipped, disabled, in a red-phase, or in a green-phase state) must be removed entirely rather than relabeled.' Ledger entries 71 (4.1), 77 (4.2), 84 (4.3), 4.6 (2026-07-13T11:15:48.881Z) record prior occurrences; 4.6 deduced non-recurrence.

observations.summary: The 4.6 update_step to review-tests is working — the 6 always-skipped tests (including the 2 onboarding.spec.ts tests that persisted through 3.3, 3.4, 4.6) were converted to conditional skips rather than merely flagged.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: '6 always-skipped tests with no condition — FIXED (converted to conditional skips).' _bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-reviews/test-review-validation-4-8.md:68-102 documents the conversion: 4 it.skip() tests in platform-env-vars.integration.spec.ts converted to conditional describe gate on token availability; 2 test.skip(title, body) tests in onboarding.spec.ts converted to in-body conditional skips on env vars. The 2 onboarding.spec.ts tests (lines 232, 284) are the same tests that persisted through 3.3, 3.4, 4.6 per ledger entry 4.6 (2026-07-13T11:15:48.881Z). Playbook review-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-13T11:15:48.883Z, updatedByRun: 4.6-a1-20260713T093042) includes 'For always-skipped tests with no condition that cannot run in any CI tier, remove them directly or convert to conditional skips — do not just flag them, as they persist as dead code across runs when only flagged.' Ledger entry 4.6 records the applied update_step.

observations.summary: The 4.6 update_step to review-nfrs is working — all 3 NFR findings are LOW (below the MEDIUM threshold for deferred-work.md entries), and the step correctly did not defer them.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-nfrs: 'Findings (3 LOW, 0 MEDIUM, 0 HIGH, 0 CRITICAL)' and 'Gate Decision: PASS — All findings are LOW severity — below the MEDIUM threshold for deferred-work.md entries. No deferred-work.md updates required.' traces/4.8-a1-20260713T131507/review-nfrs.json default view confirms: 'All findings are LOW severity — below the MEDIUM threshold for deferred-work.md entries. No deferred-work.md updates required.' Playbook review-nfrs prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-13T11:15:48.883Z, updatedByRun: 4.6-a1-20260713T093042) includes 'For any MEDIUM or higher finding that is not fixed in this step, record it in @_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md so a future story whose scope touches the affected code can pick it up.' Ledger entry 4.6 records the applied update_step.

observations.summary: The 3.9 update_step is working — implement-story proactively applied NFR patterns; the 3 LOW NFR findings are content-specific gaps (missing POSTGRES_PASSWORD in credential list, dead-code token guards), not gaps in applying documented patterns.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-nfrs: 3 LOW findings — (1) POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test in runbook not in CREDENTIAL_ENV_VARS list, (2) Vercel token prefix guard (vcp_) is a guess/dead code, (3) Railway token fragment guard duplicated/already deferred. traces/4.8-a1-20260713T131507/review-nfrs.json: 'The dev agent's NFR verification (line 343) correctly identified credential isolation and input injection as the applicable NFR categories, but missed the POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test gap and the two dead-code token guards. These are minor gaps in a documentation story where the practical risk is zero.' The implement-story step applied the uniform guard template (6 credential-isolation + 4 input-injection tests) per the prepare-tests scaffolding. Playbook implement-story prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-12T15:50:56.335Z) includes 'Before marking the story complete, re-read project-context.md and verify each non-functional pattern documented there has been applied to your changes where applicable.' Ledger entries 5, 37, 43, 52, 59, 4.1, 4.2 record prior occurrences; 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 4.1, 4.2 deduce non-recurrence.

observations.summary: The 3.11 update_step is working — prepare-tests amended Task 6.2 from 'Create a regression guard test' to 'Activate the existing regression guard test by removing test.skip() markers'; validate-story-2 caught and fixed a residual contradiction in the File Structure Requirements table.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for prepare-tests: 'Story file updated — Task 6.2 amended from Create a regression guard test to Activate the existing regression guard test by removing test.skip() markers.' Journal step_end for validate-story-2: 'Fix applied (1 critical — DP-2: internal contradiction) — File Structure Requirements table listed the regression guard test as CREATE (NEW) while Task 6.2 (amended by the ATDD checklist) says activate the existing file. The actual codebase confirms 31 test.skip() blocks already exist.' Playbook prepare-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-06T12:26:33.858Z, updatedByRun: 3.11-a1-20260706T082713) includes 'update the story file's tasks to reflect what was already done — tasks that instruct the dev to create scaffolding that prepare-tests has already applied should be amended to instruct activation of the existing scaffolding instead.' Ledger entries 38, 55 record prior occurrences; 3.9, 3.10, 3.12, 4.1, 4.2 deduce non-recurrence.

observations.summary: The 3.7 update_step is working — prepare-tests performed the browser-level mock feasibility check and correctly deferred all ACs (platform infrastructure behavior: Vercel/Railway deployment mechanisms, Postgres migration state, container health checks).
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for prepare-tests: 'E2E deferral — verified no browser-level mock can simulate any AC (all involve platform infrastructure behavior: Vercel/Railway deployment mechanisms, Postgres migration state, container health checks). Recorded in the ATDD checklist.' Journal step_end for e2e-tests: 'Story 4.8 is a documentation + verification story — not a feature story with a UI or API surface. The story's own Testing Approach explicitly excludes E2E tests.' Playbook prepare-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-06T12:26:33.858Z) includes 'Before deferring E2E coverage, verify no browser-level mock pattern can simulate the scenario — only defer if no mock covers the ACs, and record the check in the ATDD checklist.' Ledger entry 33 (3.7) records original observation; 3.11, 3.12, 4.1, 4.2 deduce non-recurrence.

observations.summary: The 4.3 update_step fixed the prompt language, but the agent's actual search execution is still inconsistent across runs — 4.6's review-tests claimed comprehensive coverage (~110 files, all types) and reported 0 stale markers, but 4.8 found 3 stale markers in files that existed during 4.6's run; the 4.6 trace has 0 mentions of those files.
observations.evidence: 4.8 review-tests validation report (_bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-reviews/test-review-validation-4-8.md:118-122) found 3 stale markers in files from earlier stories: artifacts/page.test.tsx:9 ('GREEN PHASE:' from Story 2.5), repo-size.spec.ts:13 ('Red-phase status:'), sse-back-pressure.spec.ts:37 ('red-phase coordination gap'). 4.6 review-tests default view (traces/4.6-a1-20260713T093042/review-tests.json) claims 'I searched all test directories and all test file types — approximately 110 test files' and reports 'Stale Transitional Markers — 0 FOUND (nothing to fix). All markers are accurate.' But --grep for 'page.test.tsx|repo-size|sse-back-pressure|GREEN PHASE|Red-phase|red-phase' across all 134 parts (including --include machinery) returns 0 matches — the 4.6 agent never mentioned these files or markers. The 'GREEN PHASE:' marker in page.test.tsx has existed since Story 2.5 was completed (long before 4.6's run). Playbook review-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-12T15:50:56.335Z, updatedByRun: 4.3-a1-20260712T082910) says 'Search all test directories in the project and all test file types (including component test files), not just the story's own test files.' Ledger entry 4.3 (2026-07-12T15:50:56.334Z) records the original observation and applied update_step.
observations.hypothesis: The 4.3 update_step fixed the prompt language directing comprehensive search, but the agent's actual search execution varies in thoroughness across runs — 4.6 claimed comprehensive coverage but its search tool calls did not surface the 3 files where 4.8 found markers. The prompt directs what to search but does not require the agent to verify or report which files were actually examined, so a claim of 'searched all files' is not verifiable from the output alone.
observations.nextStep: Widen into traces/4.6-a1-20260713T093042/review-tests.json with --include machinery --full to see what search method (grep/glob tool calls) the 4.6 agent used and why it missed the 3 files; compare the search method against 4.8's (which found the markers across 120 files). If the search methods differ, an update_step to review-tests specifying that the agent should list all files searched before reporting results may be warranted — making the search verifiable rather than a bare claim.
